Assessment
Claude · auto-generated

# Portfolio Credit Quality Review — Q2 2026

Portfolio delinquency improved modestly from Q1, with Order's 30–60 DPD declining 16 basis points to 0.62% despite elevated 60–90 balances at 0.83%, and Koverly holding clean at 0.08% 30–60 DPD. Lawtrades operates with structural payment delays built into its underwriting model. BRKR.IO emerged as the quarter's new pressure point, jumping from near-zero to 13.73% 30–60 DPD month-over-month on a $942K portfolio, while Flychain's 2.66% 60–90 DPD suggests developing stress in a smaller $2.8M book.

Order carries the largest leading balance at $5.5M with $447K confirmed past due, concentrated in obligors like HPI CANNA INC. ($70K, 46 days late) and Maitri Genetics ($28K, 34 days late). BRKR.IO presents $438K in leading exposure, anchored by Simple Social ($101K, 26 days late) and Strega Entertainment ($66K, 18 days late). Cledara Limited has emerged with $224K leading balance and zero confirmed collections, a shift worth monitoring. Tab Commerce, Koverly, and NurseIO carry smaller leading positions under $118K each.

Construction and Real Estate sectors show stress concentrations, with Construction at 24.38% 30+ DPD delinquency on $1.1M and Real Estate at 22.71% on $1.0M, reflecting broader commercial real estate headwinds rather than partner-specific issues. Professional Services deteriorated to 8.58% delinquency on $2.9M, driven primarily by Lawtrades' obligor base and BRKR.IO placements. Health Care and Wholesale Trade remain stable in the 5–6% range, while Retail shows a split with one cohort at 7.49% and another at 3.34%, suggesting segmentation by obligor type or partner sourcing.

The largest 30+ DPD exposures cluster within Relay ($1.05M across six obligors including Bethlehem Apparatus at $139K and Victory Doors at $110K) and Order ($291K split between HPI Canna at $231K and Northeast Extracts at $60K). Flychain carries $273K in two senior living facilities (Camelot Chateau and Noble House, each at $100K), both healthcare obligors signaling potential sector-wide pressure. BRKR.IO's Venus Brands sits at $128K delinquent, while Lawtrades' Ironclad obligation reached $67K. No single obligor exceeds $231K, limiting concentration risk, though three Order obligors and three Relay obligors each represent material tail exposure.

Over the trailing twelve months, $5.4M principal charged off across nine partners, with Koverly ($1.96M) and Order ($1.45M) accounting for 63% of losses. Avalara's charge-off of $1.22M represents a single large obligor event rather than portfolio deterioration. Flychain's $459K charge-off against a $2.8M current portfolio (16% historical loss rate) and Tab Commerce's $165K write-off suggest mature seasoning risk in smaller cohorts. The remaining five partners each charged off under $110K, with no pattern of repeat obligor names across the charge-off and current delinquent lists.

30+ DPD: —
Cohort loss rate trend — trailing 3 / 6 / 12 matured-cohort loss % (dollar-weighted)

Each point = trailing N fully matured origination cohorts aggregated as total 120+ DPD losses ÷ total originated. A cohort is matured once its latest-maturing loan has cleared the 120-day loss window, so the series ends before today; more recent cohorts are still developing losses and are excluded.

Max possible loss by cohort — 30+ DPD ceiling vs booked 120+ loss (short lag)

Every eventual 120+ loss first passes through 30 DPD, so a cohort’s current 30+ DPD balance plus already-booked losses is the ceiling on what it can lose — observable ~120 days after origination, about 3 months sooner than confirmed loss. Bars = originated ($K). Red = max possible loss % (ceiling). Black dashed = booked 120+ loss %. The gap is loans still in flight; it closes as the cohort matures.

Loss % by origination cohort — cumulative losses ÷ originated ($K stacked bars, loss % line)

Bars = originated ($K) per cohort month. Lines on right axis = % of that cohort’s originated balance currently 30–60 / 60–90 / 90–120 DPD (amber/orange/red) and cumulative loss % (black). Recent cohorts understated as losses accrue over time.

Loss by underwriting vintage — initial line assigned vs 120+ DPD balance as % of line

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total credit line assigned at first underwrite ($K). Red line = cumulative loss % of line assigned (right axis). Recent vintages understated as losses take time to develop.

Loss by underwriting vintage — total borrowed vs 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed

X-axis = month of initial underwrite decision. Bars = total amount actually borrowed (sum of original_amount across all loans) by orgs in each vintage cohort ($K). Red line = cumulative 120+ DPD balance as % of total borrowed (right axis). Denominator is realized borrowing, not credit line capacity. Recent vintages understated.

Loss ratio by origination cohort — cumulative 120+ DPD losses ÷ cumulative income collected

Bars = income collected from each cohort’s loans ($K): interest + fees + late fees + non-accrual items. Red line = cumulative loss % of cumulative income (right axis). Dashed orange line = monthly loss ratio (non-cumulative). Recent cohorts understated as both losses and income continue to accrue.

Monthly charge-offs ($K principal)
OatFi Credit Risk  ·  DPD methodology: cutoffdate − mat_dt, curr_upb > 0. Leading indicator: past partner due_date, not yet past OatFi mat_dt. Loss rate: first 120 DPD crossing or chargeoff ÷ trailing 12m originations × 12. Cohort loss %: 120+ DPD losses ÷ originated, matured cohorts only (latest loan past maturity + 120d). Utilization >120% excluded. Partners and orgs under $10K excluded.
